revision.text:
[[ja:池尻2号墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ikejiri No. 2 Kofun |image = |Other name = |part_of = Heisō Lake Kofun Group (Ikejiri Subgroup) |location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|qid=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa|1=Kakogawa, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Heisōchō|ja|平荘町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisōchō|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17215850}} Sato |coordinates = {{Coord|34.79472222222222|N|134.85797222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinates_Min = 47 |coordinates_Sec = 41.00 |coordinates_Min = 51 |coordinates_Sec = 28.65 |omitted = JP-28 |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
（Possibly a pit-type horizontal entrance stone chamber?）
（Wooden coffin inside?） |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse gear, farming tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Accompanying Tombs = |built = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |Interred Individual = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible designation1 = |notes = Mound is buried |map = Japan Hyōgo |type = |map_dot_label = Ikejiri No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top }} [[File:Lake Heiso, teibou.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Heisō Lake Eastern Embankment}}]] '''Ikejiri No. 2 Kofun''' (池尻2号墳) was an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|qid=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa|1=Kakogawa, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, in the Sato area of {{ill|Heisōchō|ja|平荘町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisōchō|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17215850}}. It was part of the Heisō Lake Kofun Group (Ikejiri Subgroup). The mound is now buried. == Overview == This kofun was built independently on the northern foothills of {{ill|Jizōji-yama|ja|地蔵寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizōji-yama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in southern Hyōgo Prefecture. The mound was severely damaged, and an excavation was carried out in 1964 (Shōwa 39). It was later buried beneath the eastern embankment of Heisō Lake. The tomb type remains unclear due to destruction. During the investigation, a rectangular mound measuring 11 meters east-west, 6.45 meters north-south, and 1.8 meters high was confirmed{{Sfn|History of Kakogawa, Vol. 4, Historical Records I|1996}}. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and house-shaped haniwa were discovered on the mound surface{{Sfn|History of Kakogawa, Vol. 4, Historical Records I|1996}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented northwest-southeast, with a possibility of being a pit-type horizontal entrance chamber{{Sfn|History of Kakogawa, Vol. 4, Historical Records I|1996}}. Excavations uncovered weapons, armor, horse gear, farming tools, and Sue pottery. Notably, the Sue pottery included rare double-spouted vessels and ear-handled goblets, sharing features with Korean Peninsula pottery. Additionally, the horse bit resembled an early "F"-shaped mirror plate, making it a significant find{{Sfn|History of Kakogawa, Vol. 4, Historical Records I|1996}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 5th century, during the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. == burial == The burial facility is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented northwest-southeast, measuring 4.51 meters in length, 1.18 meters in width, and 0.61 meters in height{{Sfn|History of Kakogawa, Vol. 4, Historical Records I|1996}}. The stone chamber is built using five to six layers of angular gravel for the long side walls, while the short ends consist of upright stone slabs topped with one to two layers of angular gravel. The floor is covered with clay and small pebbles, with traces of red pigment scattered across it. A 10 cm-wide groove along the walls lacks pebbles. The ceiling consists of four stone slabs{{Sfn|History of Kakogawa, Vol. 4, Historical Records I|1996}}. The construction of the short end walls suggests the possibility that this was a pit-type horizontal entrance stone chamber{{Sfn|History of Kakogawa, Vol. 4, Historical Records I|1996}}. Numerous nails were found inside the chamber, indicating the presence of a wooden coffin measuring 2.55 meters in length and 0.7 meters in width, with the head facing northeast. Alongside the walls, iron arrowheads, armor, horse gear, farming tools, and Sue pottery were placed. Swords and spears were positioned near the northeast gravel lining, while a double-spouted vessel was specially placed on a stone slab{{Sfn|History of Kakogawa, Vol. 4, Historical Records I|1996}}. == burial goods == [[File:池尻2号墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Helmet with a protruding crest}}{{small|Exhibited at Kakogawa General Cultural Center Museum (same for other images).}}]] [[File:池尻2号墳出土 双はそう.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sue pottery double-spouted vessel}}]] The 1964 excavation of the stone chamber uncovered the following burial goods{{Sfn|History of Kakogawa, Vol. 4, Historical Records I|1996}}: * '''Weapons''' ** Three iron swords - Long swords with deer antler-decorated hilts. ** Three iron spears. ** Over 45 iron arrowheads - Four flat-based, many pointed-based. * '''Armor''' ** Riveted helmet with a protruding crest. ** Shoulder guards (missing). ** Neck guards (missing). * '''Horse Gear''' ** Wooden-core, iron-plated stirrups. ** Bits. ** Tail fittings. ** Connecting metal fittings. ** Decorative metal fittings. ** Iron rings. * '''Farming Tools''' ** U-shaped plow tip. ** Iron axe. ** Square awl. ** Cone-shaped awl. * '''Sue pottery''' - Three double-spouted vessels, two goblets. * '''Other''' - Nails, brackets. File:池尻2号墳出土 鉄剣・鉄釘・鎹・鉄鏃・錐.JPG|Iron swords, nails, brackets, arrowheads, awl File:池尻2号墳出土 馬具.JPG|Horse gear File:池尻2号墳出土 鉄鉾・鉄斧・鋤先.JPG|Iron spears, iron axe, plow tip File:池尻2号墳出土 須恵器 はそう.JPG|Sue pottery double-spouted vessel File:池尻2号墳出土 須恵器 高坏.JPG|Sue pottery goblet == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kakogawa General Cultural Center Museum|ja|加古川総合文化センター博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa General Cultural Center Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hiraoka-chō, Kakogawa) - Houses and exhibits burial goods from Ikejiri No. 2 Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Local Government Publications ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=Heisō Lake Kofun Group|title=Hyōgo Prefecture History: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Hyōgo Prefecture|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Hyōgo Prefecture History: Archaeological Materials|1992}}}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Ikejiri No. 2 Kofun|title=History of Kakogawa, Vol. 4, Historical Records I|publisher=Kakogawa City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|History of Kakogawa, Vol. 4, Historical Records I|1996}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Heisō Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisō Lake Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505743|1=Heisō Lake Kofun Group}} * [[Horned Kabuto]] 池尻2号墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Ikejiri Kofun No. 2|ja|池尻2号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikejiri Kofun No. 2|qq=}}
{{small|Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. Exhibited at the Kakogawa Cu ... 13 KB (1,542 words) - 07:20, 19 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|池尻2号墳|version=93113182|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:池尻2号墳]] {{wikidata link|Q122833158}} [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
